2.Conference Proceedings/1.YangIEEENEMS2.title: Fabrication Ultrasonic Microdevice Microparticles Separation
2.Conference Proceedings/1.YangIEEENEMS2.abstract: In this paper a new microdevice for on-chip separation of microparticles is presented, in which a micromachined silicon nitride membrane structure is bonded with a glass substrate and then PZT plates are bonded to the silicon and glass substrates to excite the microdevice into vibration. Due to the energy coupling, silicon nitride membranes are excited into vibration as well, generating an ultrasonic standing wave field below the membranes, for which we can use it to separate microparticles within the fluids. In the paper, we start from the radiation force analysis and then design and fabricate the practical microdevice, and use finite element method in ANSYS to analyze its separation performance.

2.Conference Proceedings/10.YangTransduc.title: HIGH-PERFORMANCE PROTEIN PRECONCENTRATOR USING MICROCHANNEL-INTEGRATED NAFION STRIP H. Yang, M. Shen, V. Sivagnanam and M. A. M. Gijs Laboratory of Microsystems, École Polytechnique Fédérale de Lausanne, Lausanne, SWITZERLAND A microfluidic device for protein preconcentration based on the electrokin
2.Conference Proceedings/10.YangTransduc.abstract: A microfluidic device for protein preconcentration based on the electrokinetic trapping principle is proposed. It comprises a Nafion strip which is simply cut from a commercially available membrane and integrated into a polydimethylsiloxane (PDMS) microfluidic structure using a guiding channel. By applying low voltage (<15 V), the device exhibits a preconcentration factor of >103 of fluorescently labeled bovine serum albumin (BSA) within a few minutes. Moreover, a numerical study of the preconcentration effect is presented to microscopically understand the phenomenon in our type of device.

2.Conference Proceedings/11.YangMicroTAS.title: SELF-ASSEMBLED MELAMINE MICROLENS ARRAYS FOR IMMUNOFLUORESCENCE ENHANCEMENT
2.Conference Proceedings/11.YangMicroTAS.abstract: Self-assembled dielectric microlenses are used for focusing of the fluorescent signal emitted from a surface-based immunoassay, performed on silane micropatterns as assay substrates, to enhance the detection signal. In our model system, a fluorescent immunocomplex is formed on (3-aminopropyl)triethoxysilane (APTES) microstructures, and then carboxyl-functionalized melamine microparticles with two different sizes are electrostatically self-assembled on the microstructures. Mouse IgG diluted in phosphate buffered saline (PBS) is used as model target antigen and easily detected down to a concentration of 2 ng/mL. We also present a detailed two-dimensional numerical study using the Finite Element Method (FEM).

2.Conference Proceedings/13.CornagliaMNE.title: Desktop publishing template for MNE08 abstract book

2.Conference Proceedings/14.YangMicroTAS.title: LENS ARRAY BY ELECTROSTATIC PATTERNING OF DIELECTRIC MICROSPHERES IN A PARYLENE-C WELL TEMPLATE
2.Conference Proceedings/14.YangMicroTAS.abstract: We present the fabrication of a microlens array which can be integrated into a microfluidic device. In the demonstrated technique, a microwell array is firstly fabricated in a Parylene-C layer by a standard cleanroom process. Afterwards, the optically transparent dielectric microspheres with high refractive index are patterned inside the well template by utilizing an electrostatic technique. The mechanism of microsphere patterning is studied, and the lens effect of the microsphere is experimentally verified in a water-based medium. We also present a detailed two-dimensional numerical optical simulation study on the patterned microspheres using the Finite Element Method (FEM).

2.Conference Proceedings/15.YangProcSPIE.title: PROCEEDINGS OF SPIE
2.Conference Proceedings/15.YangProcSPIE.abstract: We propose the use of a high-refractive index glass microsphere in combination with a conventional fluorescence microscope for imaging sub-cellular organelles and biomolecules. The microsphere is placed on a sample that is immersed in water, collects the near-field nano-features of the sample and generates a magnified virtual image in the far- field, which is recorded through a conventional water immersion objective. We first investigate the imaging capability of the microspheres on size-calibrated fluorescent micro-/nano-particles. The experimental results obtained from a microsphere with 60 μm in diameter demonstrate imaging capability of features of ~λ/7-size (λ is the wavelength) with a magnification factor of 5.4. The position of the virtual image, the field-of-view (FOV) of the microsphere and the magnification factor are studied by using microspheres with different sizes. Finite Element Method (FEM) simulations are performed, providing key insight into the imaging effect of the microspheres with super-resolution capability. Moreover, the distribution and complex shape of different sub-cellular organelles, like centrioles, mitochondria and chromosomes in the AML12 cell line, are imaged with help of the glass microspheres. Thereafter, the subcellular location of mitochondrial encoded proteins could be studied.

2.Conference Proceedings/16.YangProcSPIE.title: PROCEEDINGS OF SPIE
2.Conference Proceedings/16.YangProcSPIE.abstract: We demonstrate a method to determine the Brownian motion and the diffusion coefficient of a nanoparticle in water in a plane that is parallel to a solid boundary and as function of the distance normal to that boundary by using an optical tweezers instrument. A solution of 190 nm-diameter fluorescent polystyrene nanoparticles in de-ionized (DI) water is introduced in a micro-chamber built from two thin glass substrates. A single particle is trapped by the tweezers and optically moved in the z-direction normal to a substrate. By analyzing a scatter plot of the time-dependent positions of the nanoparticle in the x-y plane in a histogram, the diffusion coefficient parallel to the substrate of the Brownian particle constrained by the substrate is determined as a function of the distance between the substrate and the nanoparticle. The experimental results indicate the increased drag effect on the nanoparticle when it is close to the substrate, as evidenced by an experimental diffusion coefficient nearby the substrate that is about half of that of the particle in the bulk fluid.

2.Conference Proceedings/17.YangMicroTAS.title: 1843_T.527g
2.Conference Proceedings/17.YangMicroTAS.abstract: We present the use of a microlens array in combination with a conventional optical microscope set-up for the detection of single nanoparticles (NPs) in fluid medium. Optically transparent dielectric microspheres are patterned in a microfabricated well array template and used as microlenses focusing the light originating from a microscope objective into so-called photonic nanojets that expose the medium within the microfluidic channel. When the NPs pass the nanojets, the detection signal is highly enhanced, Au NPs with size down to 50 nm, fluorescent NPs down to 20 nm in size, as well as biomolecule-linked NPs are clearly observed.

2.Conference Proceedings/18.HuszkaTransd.title: DIELECTRIC MICROSPHERE-BASED OPTICAL SYSTEM FOR SUPER-RESOLUTION MICROSCOPY
2.Conference Proceedings/18.HuszkaTransd.abstract: We present a dielectric microsphere-based super- resolution optical microscope that can image a sample beyond the classical diffraction limit [1]. The super- resolution imaging capability of a single microsphere normally is restricted to an area of ~10 μm2, which essentially is the area of close contact of a microsphere with the sample. By fixing the microsphere on a frame attached to a microscope objective, we perform automatic step-by- step scanning and imaging of the sample. Later, the generated image set is synthesized into a single wide-field super-resolution image using a dedicated algorithm. Furthermore, we propose an array fabrication process for placing multiple microspheres in the optical path of the microscope objective, thereby parallelizing the imaging process.

2.Conference Proceedings/19.IEEE MEMS 20.title: Magnetic Force-Based Microfluidic Chip for Plant Seed Levitation to Simulate Microgravity Environment
2.Conference Proceedings/19.IEEE MEMS 20.abstract: For plants on Earth, gravitropism is essential for seedling establishment and plant growth. As an integrated part of plant development, gravitropic response is mainly mediated by changes of photohormone auxin. Study on auxin responses in microgravity condition is critical for illustrating how plants perceive environmental gravity conditions and then initiating auxin signaling pathway, ultimately, helping to reveal how plants response to gravity stimulus. Nevertheless, key barriers to answering these questions are the lack of a tool that can provide simulated microgravity condition. In this work, we have developed a microfluidic negative-magnetophoresis (MNM) platform to levitate Arabidopsis seeds in an equilibrium plane where magnetic force and buoyancy force are equal and opposite in directions. With the advantage of the MNM platform to simulate a microgravity environment, it’s found that auxin response is obviously decreased after seed levitation during early seed germination. This can enable the study of the physiological process and molecular mechanism of auxin in a simulated microgravity condition and benefit further applications for optimizing plant growth in space.

2.Conference Proceedings/2.GuoIEEEMEMS20.title: MEMS索引合体.indd
2.Conference Proceedings/2.GuoIEEEMEMS20.abstract: In this paper, we present a new type of betavoltaic microbattery using porous silicon pn junction device. Investigations are made on using high energy radioisotopes as the source and mechanisms of increased energy bandgap and electron trapping in porous silicon for increasing power output and energy conversion efficiency. The device is fabricated and experimental result shows that this new type of microbattery is a promising technology for developing nuclear microbatteries with the increased power output and conversion efficiency.

2.Conference Proceedings/20.IEEE MEMS 20.title: A Human Cornea-On-A-Chip
2.Conference Proceedings/20.IEEE MEMS 20.abstract: This paper describes a microfluidic platform to reconstitute human cornea and the complexity of its interface to external environment. To recapitulate the structural complexity and biological phenotype of the cornea, we embedded a collagen-coated porous membrane in a double-layer microfluidic chip for three-dimensional (3D) culture of cells derived from the human cornea. Moreover, a pressure-driven flow was used for long-term cultivation, which not only forms a bionic human cornea, but also simulates the microenvironment of the human ocular surface. We demonstrated a novel micro-engineered device that would improve the interaction between the in vitro model and the external environment, providing a potential solution to obtain predictive results to in vivo observations.

2.Conference Proceedings/21.IEEE MEMS 20.title: A High-Throughput Nanofluidic Device for Small Extracellular Vesicle Nanoporation
2.Conference Proceedings/21.IEEE MEMS 20.abstract: We propose a nanofluidic device named “small extracellular vesicle nanoporator (sEVN)” for high-throughput nanoporation of small extracellular vesicles (sEVs). By transporting sEVs through constriction nanochannel with height comparable to their dimension, transient nanopores are introduced on the sEVs membrane by mechanical membrane disruption, while maintaining sEV integrity. The sEVN consisting of 30,000 nanochannels demonstrates a high sample throughput and absence on clogging issues, and the working mechanism of the device is elucidated through experimental and numerical study. This platform provides a way for high-throughput sEV nanoporation and offers promises for engineering sEVs as universal drug delivery vehicles for biomedical applications.

2.Conference Proceedings/22.IEEE MEMS 20.title: In-Flow Extraction of RNA in Extracellular Vesicles Using a Silicon-Based Microfluidic Device
2.Conference Proceedings/22.IEEE MEMS 20.abstract: Extracellular vesicles (EVs) transport proteins, signaling lipids, and nucleic acids from donor cells to recipient cells, therefore have been verified to serve as mediators of intercellular communications. EV-resident RNAs have been widely used as biomarkers of disease and prognostic indicators. Thus, in situ extraction of RNAs in a microfluidic flow is of great importance for developing integrated microsystems to realize on-chip PCR. This paper presents a silicon-based microfluidic device for solid-phase extraction of EV-resident RNAs. The microfluidic device contains micro-pillar arrays within an area of 0.25cm2 on the silicon substrate, different structures with various surface-to-volume ratio (SVR) are investigated to compare their RNA extraction efficiencies. A 20% extraction efficiency is obtained and characterized by using a Nanodrop UV-Vis spectrophotometer. The proposed on-chip extraction of EV-resident RNAs would provide new approaches to develop integrated microfluidic systems to realize PCR for point-of-care applications.

2.Conference Proceedings/23.IEEE MEMS 20.title: Ultrahigh Resolution On-Chip Separation System Using Negative Magnetophoresis
2.Conference Proceedings/23.IEEE MEMS 20.abstract: A high-resolution magnetic separation microfluidic system used for the negative magnetophoretic separation of nanoparticles is presented in this paper. The chip can generate an ultrahigh gradient magnetic field greater than 105 T/m in the separation channel by integrating two on-chip magnetic pole arrays. A label-free separation of nanoparticle is achieved by this microfluidic system with a mixed sample of 0.2 μm and 1 μm polystyrene particles, where the recovery rate of 0.2 μm particles is 91.20%, and the purity reaches 94.72%. The experimental results show that the chip can efficiently separate nano-scale particles with ultrahigh resolution, and in future, it may develop into a versatile and robust tool for the separation and purification of nano-biological samples.

2.Conference Proceedings/24.IEEE NEMS 20.title: Photonic Nanojet Produced by A Microfluidic Channel for Biofluid Monitoring*
2.Conference Proceedings/24.IEEE NEMS 20.abstract: Owing to the properties like narrow beam waist and high intensity focusing, photonic nanojet (PNJ) has attracted sustainable attention in many fields, such as single nanoparticle or molecular detection, fluorescent or Raman scattering enhancement, subwavelength nanopatterning, super-resolution imaging, etc. The characteristics of PNJ are mainly affected by the material compositions and morphology features of the investigated microstructures. In this paper, we present a microfluidic channel structure to produce PNJs for biofluid monitoring. The proposed scheme is numerically studied by using the finite element method. Through the change of PNJ parameters, such as the maximum light intensity, working distance and full width at half-maximum, the refractive indices of the injected biofluids can be determined. I. BACKGROUND Biofluid monitoring plays an important role in the field of life science. The physical properties of biofluid such as refractive index, density, viscosity, conductivity and opacity can provide abundant information for monitoring [1]. Refractive index is a very interesting property as it contains a lot of important formation on such as blood glucose level and label-free molecular interaction [2, 3]. Optical measurement on the refractive index has attracted wide attentions owning to its reliability, fast response and high sensitivity [4]. Photonic nanojet (PNJ), a scattered beam of light with a high-intensity main lobe and very narrow waist that can be generated upon illumination on a transparent dielectric symmetric microstructure. Therefore, PNJ is a good candidate for monitoring the refractive index of liquid [5, 6], as the field distributions and shape features of the PNJ are mainly determined by the geometrical morphologies and material properties of the microstructures [7-11]. The experimental characterization of PNJ can be performed using the method proposed in Ref. [12]. Here, we design a microfluidic channel structure consisted of a glass tube

2.Conference Proceedings/25.IEEE NEMS 20.title: Magnetic Force Enabled Plant Seed Levitation to Simulate Microgravity Environment on A Microfluidic Chip
2.Conference Proceedings/25.IEEE NEMS 20.abstract: For plants on Earth, gravitropism is essential element affecting seedling establishment and plant development. As an integrated part of plant growth and development, gravitropic response is mainly mediated by alternation of photohormone auxin. In microgravity environment, the study of auxin responses is critical to illustrate how plants perceive environmental gravity conditions and then initiate auxin signaling pathway. Nevertheless, the major challenge is the lack of a tool that can provide a simulated microgravity condition. In this study, we developed a microfluidic negative-magnetophoresis (MNM) platform to levitate Arabidopsis seeds in an equilibrium plane where the applied magnetic force compensates gravitational acceleration. With the advantage of the MNM platform to mimic a microgravity environment on chip, it’s found that auxin response is significantly repressed during seed levitation. The MNM can enable the study of physiological process and molecular mechanism of hormone in a simulated microgravity condition, which benefits further applications for plant space biology.

2.Conference Proceedings/26.IEEE NEMS 20.title: A Human Cornea-on-a-Chip for the Study of Epithelial Wound Healing by Extracellular Vesicles*
2.Conference Proceedings/26.IEEE NEMS 20.abstract: We present a microfluidic platform to recapitulate human cornea's complexity and its interface with external environment. According to the cornea's anatomy, the fully integrated human cornea on the chip consists of human corneal cells and a porous membrane embedded in a microfluidic platform, replicating multi-scale structural organization and biological phenotypes. Using this system, we discover and verify the fully integrated human cornea barrier function on the chip. Besides, we develop an in vitro model of the corneal epithelial wound to study the cell-free therapy efficacy using extracellular vesicles (EVs). We find that EVs derived from bone marrow mesenchymal stem cells (BM- MSCs) can significantly accelerate corneal epithelial wound healing. A dose-dependent effect is also observed in the experiments. This work improves our ability to simulate the interaction between human cornea and external world in vitro. It may contribute to the future development of new screening platforms for biopharmaceuticals.

2.Conference Proceedings/27.IEEE NEMS 20.title: Fluorescence Enhancement Utilizing Dielectric Microbeads Semi-open Microwells
2.Conference Proceedings/27.IEEE NEMS 20.abstract: Dielectric microbeads can converge light into a narrow beam with high intensity which allows for enhancing the fluorescent signals. However, a critical challenge for the experimental realization is introducing the fluorescent samples into this extremely small beam area. Here, we design and fabricate dielectric microbeads with semi-open microwells in which localized converging light beam of high intensity can be generated. We show that fluorescent microspheres can be efficiently loaded into the semi-open microwells and thus simultaneously illuminated by the converging beam generated by the micro-optical structures without any further manipulations. Pronounced fluorescent enhancement of around 9 folds can be obtained in comparison with the fluorescent microspheres on glass substrates, stemming from the excellent convergent effect of this micro-optical structure.

2.Conference Proceedings/28.MicroTAS2021.title: A MICROFLUIDIC SYSTEM FOR LABEL-FREE AND HIGH-THROUGHPUT MAGNETIC SEPARATION OF NANOPARTICLES
2.Conference Proceedings/28.MicroTAS2021.abstract: A microfluidic system for label-free and high-throughput magnetic separation of nanoparticles is reported, and the performance of the microfluidic system is studied under different flow rates. An ultrahigh magnetic field is generated in the microfluidic channel by fabricating an on-chip integrated magnetic micro-pole array, hereby establishing a negative magnetophoretic technique to separate nanoparticles in this device. The optimal flow rate of 2.5 μL/min is obtained from both simulation and experimental results..

2.Conference Proceedings/29.MicroTAS2021.title: A HIGH-THROUGHPUT NANOFLUIDIC DEVICE FOR EXOSOME LOADING
2.Conference Proceedings/29.MicroTAS2021.abstract: This paper presents a high-throughput nanofluidic chip named “exosome nanoporator (ENP)” enabling cargo loading into exosomes. Through the nanoconfinement of the nanochannels with a height comparable to the exosome size, transient nanopores are introduced on the exosome membrane, allowing the diffusion of cargo molecules into the exosomes from the surrounding medium. The ENP device consisting of 30,000 equivalent nanochannels demonstrates a high sample throughput. ENP-treated exosomes present enhanced drug delivery capacity to tumor cells, indicating the potential opportunities in preparing drug-carrying exosomes for biomedical applications.

2.Conference Proceedings/3.YangIEEEUltra.title: P2E-2 Study of Particles Separation in the Ultrasonic Microdevice
2.Conference Proceedings/3.YangIEEEUltra.abstract: In this paper, a new MEMS device that can separate microparticles on-chip by the use of bulk-mode excited membrane vibration is proposed, designed and microfabricated. By starting from the one-dimensional analytical mode of forces acting on particles,the force for ultrasonically separating particles is profoundly studied, and finite element method in ANSYS is used to analyze the vibration modes, the acoustic field and flow field in the microdevice due to the membrane vibration. With these analyses, we obtain the ultrasonic radiation force acting on different kinds of particles in the water, blood plasma and milk medium when the microdevice vibrates at different frequencies and then study their separation.

2.Conference Proceedings/30.MicroTAS2021.title: LOCALIZED PHOTONIC NANOJET ENABLED QUANTITATIVE OPTICAL ENHANCEMENT
2.Conference Proceedings/30.MicroTAS2021.abstract: Method of developing quantitative optical enhancement through photonic nanojet (PNJ) is previously undescribed. In this work, we localize PNJ in a semi-open microwell by utilizing dielectric microsphere based optical microstructures. We demonstrate that individual fluorescent microsphere can be introduced to the designated position in the PNJ and generates reproducible enhanced fluorescence signals, achieving quantitative optical enhancement.

2.Conference Proceedings/31.IEEE NEMS 20.title: Detection of Molecules Based on Enhanced Backscattering Effect in Microsphere Lens

2.Conference Proceedings/32.MicroTAS2022.title: MESENCHYMAL STEM CELL-DERIVED EXTRACELLULAR VESICLES PROMOTE REPAIRING OF CORNEAL WOUND IN HUMAN CORNEA-ON-A-CHIP
2.Conference Proceedings/32.MicroTAS2022.abstract: In this work, a microfluidic platform consisting of human corneal cells and a porous membrane was developed, replicating the multi-scale structural organization and biological phenotype of human cornea. Using this human cornea-on-a-chip, we develop an in vitro model of the corneal epithelial wound to study the cell-free therapy efficacy using extracellular vesicles (EVs).

2.Conference Proceedings/33.MicroTAS2022.title: A MICROFLUIDC SYSTEM FOR LABEL-FREE AND BIO-COMPATIBLE MAGNETIC SEPARATION OF SMALL EXTRACELLULAR VESICLES
2.Conference Proceedings/33.MicroTAS2022.abstract: A microfluidic system for label-free and biocompatible magnetic separation of small extracellular vesicles is reported. By using the on-chip magnetic pole arrays, the magnetic field gradient in the microchannel is drastically increased. Therefore, the on-chip negative magnetophoretic separation with nanoscale resolution is realized using the ferrofluid of extremely low concentration. In the experiments, the sEVs are successfully separated from cell culture supernatant, the recovery rate and purity of sEVs are 85.80% and 80.45%, superiorly exceeding the performance that ultracentrifugation method can provide.

2.Conference Proceedings/34.MicroTAS2022.title: A MICROFLUIDIC DEVICE TO PROMOTE EXOSOME SECRETION
2.Conference Proceedings/34.MicroTAS2022.abstract: In this paper, we propose a high-throughput microfluidic device to promote exosome secretion from mesenchymal stem cells. Through a microchannel with ridge gap height smaller than cell size, mechanical stimulation acting on the cells when they pass through the ridge gaps can effectively permeabilize the cell membrane, thus boosting the cells to secrete exosomes into extracellular space. The results show that the secretion amount of exosomes from the cells treated by the microfluidic device increases by 3.1-fold without affecting their bio-activity, thus providing a potential platform to improve exosome yield for biomedical research.

2.Conference Proceedings/35.MicroTAS2022.title: MICROSPHERE ASSISTED LIGHT-SCATTERING IMAGING OF PLASMONIC NANOPARTICLES AT THE SINGLE PARTICLE LEVEL
2.Conference Proceedings/35.MicroTAS2022.abstract: We present a microsphere assisted imaging approach for recording the scattered light of a single plasmonic nanoparticle. Upon the utilization of microspheres, single gold nanoparticle with diameter below the classic optical diffraction limit can be imaged under the conventional light microscope.

2.Conference Proceedings/36.CSEV2022_胡师..title: Highly Efficient Labelling of Extracellular Vesicles for Enhanced Detection on a Microfluidic Platform Shi Hu, Rui Hao, Zitong Yu, Huitao Zhang, Hui Yang* Bionic Sensing and Intelligence Center, Institute of Biomedical and Health Engineering, Shenzhen Institute of Advanced Technology, Chinese Academ
2.Conference Proceedings/36.CSEV2022_胡师..abstract: Developing precise extracellular vesicle (EV) labelling techniques with minimal disturbance is of great importance to the follow-up EV detection and analysis. Here we introduce a microfluidic platform for enhancing EVs’ labelling efficiency. The platform provides an excellent sample throughput and possesses the merits of a higher labelling efficiency with 50% relative improvement and lower background noise compared to conventional co-incubation approach. The characterization results showed that our EV labelling platform could maintain the size and morphology of EVs, holding good potentials in the precise labelling of EVs, which is essential to the subsequent EV detection and analysis.

2.Conference Proceedings/37.CSEV2022_郝锐..abstract: This paper reports a high-throughput microfluidic device to promote exosome secretion from mesenchymal stem cells. Through a microchannel with ridge gap height smaller than cell size, mechanical stimulation acting on the cells when they pass through the squeezing ridges can effectively permeabilize the cell membrane, thus boosting the cells to secrete exosomes into extracellular space. The results show that the secretion amount of exosomes from the cells treated by the microfluidic device increases by 3.1-fold without affecting their bio- activity, thus providing a potential platform to improve exosome yield for biomedical research.

2.Conference Proceedings/38.IEEE ACP2022.title: Ultrasensitive Plasmonic Biosensing Based on Critical Tuning of Phase Change Materials
2.Conference Proceedings/38.IEEE ACP2022.abstract: Here, we have revealed the potential of exploiting phase change materials (PCM) for ultrasensitive plasmonic biosensing through simulations and theoretical analysis. We have demonstrated that the detection sensitivity of the plasmonic sensors can be optimized and significantly improved by precisely engineering the configuration of PCM on the plasmonic sensing substrate and inducing a giant lateral position shift. These results will provide new opportunities for the ultrasensitive detection of biomolecules in various application scenarios.

2.Conference Proceedings/39.IEEE ACP2022.title: Subwavelength Nano-imaging with a Microbottle Lens
2.Conference Proceedings/39.IEEE ACP2022.abstract: A dielectric microbottle lens constituted by circular glass microcylinder and parabolic-shaped polymer covering layer is demonstrated here. The microbottle lens is fabricated by transferring, wetting and photocuring of UV-curable polymer on a tapered optical microfiber. By using such microbottle lens, periodic grating structures with subwavelength pitch of 360 nm can be imaged in two different imaging modes. When the pitch of the grating structures decreases to 260 nm, clear imaging of the grating structures can only be achieved in the case that the grating stripes are parallel to the microfiber axis of the microbottle lens. The results are analyzed and discussed by finite element simulation.

2.Conference Proceedings/4.YangIEEEUltra.title: P6H-8 Design of a Bulk-Micromachined Piezoelectric Accelerometer
2.Conference Proceedings/4.YangIEEEUltra.abstract: A bulk-micromachined piezoelectric accelerometer is proposed and designed, in which the bulk-micromachined silicon seismic mass is suspended by the cantilevers and part of cantilever is covered with ZnO piezoelectric thin film. In this paper, we first derive the one-dimensional analytical model of the piezoelectric cantilever to study the factors affecting the sensitivity of the accelerometer, and then design the piezoelectric accelerometers with straight and folded cantilever structures. Totally, 12 different structures of the microaccelemeter are investigated. The results show that this type of bulk- micromachined piezoelectric accelerometer can own high sensitivity and wide working range to meet requirement of some applications in aerospace and mechanical shock inspection for vehicles.

2.Conference Proceedings/40.IEEE ACP2022.title: A Simulation Study of Photonic Nanojet Generated by a Combination of a Dielectric Microsphere and a Hemisphere Lens
2.Conference Proceedings/40.IEEE ACP2022.abstract: Photonic nanojet (PNJ) is a highly focused light beam generated on the shadow side surface of a plane-wave- illuminated dielectric microcylinder, microsphere or hemisphere lens. Compound microsphere lenses allow image magnification and better resolution. Here, we report the PNJ generated by using a combination of a dielectric microsphere lens and a hemisphere lens. The numerical simulation results demonstrate that full width at half-maximum (FWHM) and working distance (WD) show a decreasing trend with the increases of the distance between the microsphere and the hemisphere. Compared with the hemisphere lens of smaller radius, the hemisphere lens with larger radius has greater FWHM and WD. The minimum FWHM is ~0.82λ and the corresponding WD is ~2λ.

2.Conference Proceedings/41.IEEE CLEO202.title: Exploiting 2D MXene nanosheets for ultrasensitive plasmonic biosensing
2.Conference Proceedings/41.IEEE CLEO202.abstract: We revealed the potential of exploiting 2D MXene nanosheets as plasmonic substrates for enhanced biosensing. The detection of BSA molecules based on MXene-on-Au substrate was successfully demonstrated with a detection limit down to 10 fM.

2.Conference Proceedings/5.YangMicroNano.title: On the Ultrasonic Manipulation of Micro/Nano Particles
2.Conference Proceedings/5.YangMicroNano.abstract: In this paper, we study the ultrasonic manipulation of micro/nano in an ultrasonic standing wave field. Starting from the forces acting on particles in stationary standing wave field, we studied the factors affecting ultrasonic manipulation of micro/nano particles within the fluidic medium. Based on analyzing particles’ motion in an ultrasound – gravity coupled field, we proposed and discussed a new application using ultrasonic manipulation of particles to categorize different kinds of micro/nano particles in fluidic medium.

2.Conference Proceedings/6.GuoMicroNano2.title: Design and Fabrication of a Micro-Knife for Minimally Invasive Surgery
2.Conference Proceedings/6.GuoMicroNano2.abstract: In this paper, a micro-knife for minimally invasive surgery is proposed. It is a silicon horn shape and works in an ultrasonic longitudinal vibration mode to realize the precise incision with its sharp tip at one end. Finite element method in ANSYS is used to analyze and design the device to obtain the vibration frequency and displacement amplitude of the longitudinal mode. In the end, microfabrication of the proposed micro-knife is presented.

2.Conference Proceedings/7.GuoISAPE2008..title: Preparation of Papers in Two-Column Format for the Proceedings of the 2004 Sarnoff Symposium
2.Conference Proceedings/7.GuoISAPE2008..abstract: In this paper, we present an analytical model of the acoustic damping of a four-clamped membrane in a ultrasonic microelectromechanical system device. The model is used to analyze effects of acoustic damping on the dynamic behavior of the microdevice, in which four-clamped membranes vibrate at ultrasonic frequency, as well as a flow goes through the cavity under the membrane. A modified Reynolds equation that is derived from the Navier-Stokes equation is used to model the flow under the membrane. The analytical solutions for the damping pressure and force are obtained. Factors that affect the damping pressure and force are discussed. Furthermore, the result of the microdevice’s performance form analytical calculation is compared with that from the finite element method in ANSYS.

2.Conference Proceedings/9.SivagnanamAIP.title: Title Goes Here
2.Conference Proceedings/9.SivagnanamAIP.abstract: . We performed a direct immunoassay inside a microfluidic channel on patterned streptavidin-coated beads, which captured fluorescently-labeled biotin target molecules from a continuous flow. We arranged the beads in a dot array at the bottom of the channel and demonstrated their position- and flow rate-dependent fluorescence. As the target analyte gets gradually depleted from the flow when passing downstream the channel, the highest fluorescence intensity was observed on the most upstream positioned dot patterns. We propose a simple analytical convection model to explain this spatio-temporal fluorescence.
